channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: St barnabas hall bus stop (DP) is really in need of repairing as the road has multiple potholes, 4th July
item.description:
St barnabas hall bus stop (DP) is really in need of repairing as the road has multiple potholes and is getting worse. Has need like this for at least 1 year

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Downham Way, Downham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
Fly tips. Domestic waste

item.title: Plastic and wood builders' waste flytipped outside 49 Ashburnham Grove, SE10 8UJ, 4th July
item.description:
Plastic and wood builders' waste dumped outside 49 Ashburnham Grove, SE10 8UJ for past 5 days. This keep happening outside this address.

item.title: Weeds, 4th July
item.description:
Please arrange to remove over grown weeds along the road and Abbotts Walk. See photo Thank you

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Leckwith Avenue, West Heath, Greater London

item.title: Bikes blocking access, 4th July
item.description:
Voi have been overloading the e bike parking bay, and leaving bikes outside of the parking bay. This is blocking access to bonita mews. This is third time I have reported this.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): St Asaph Road, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Event organiser lack common sense, 4th July
item.description:
I don't really understand how so often you can organize these events and still you have dead ends. Basically areas where the fence is blocking one into the path off but not the other and there's no signage at the open end of the path so you only realize it's a dead end when you get to the end of it. There's some serious lack of common sense with the people that run this park can do these events.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ravensbourne Greenway, Southend, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Builders waste in front of 9 Charleston Street, 4th July
item.description:
Builders waste has been on the pavement for a few days blocking the path and spreading across the street

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Charleston Street, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped fence., 4th July
item.description:

item.title: Bins overflowing, 4th July
item.description:
Unemptied rubbish is fast accumulating, is spilling over dangerously onto pedestrian paved area, causing a smell too. Urgent litter clearing needed please.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Friary Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bins not emptied, 4th July
item.description:

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Mark Street, Hackney, London

item.title: Two wheeled degeneracy left in the park, 4th July
item.description:
Just one of many of these bikes left all over the park I don't know why you allow these companies to let people use them in the park and just trapped degenerates people that are doing drug deals and antisocial behavior.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Beckenham Hill Road, Southend, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Palace Road, Streatham Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Constant littering and dumped rubbish, 4th July
item.description:
I did a clean up down here as a street friend but it never stays tidy. Football supporters and cricketers, parents watching their offspring, parked cars etc mean so much rubbish is constantly discarded. Two large venues in Crystal Palace training ground and Kent County Cricket so contact them and ask them both to be more community minded and help clear up the rubbish and remind their visitors to take their rubbish home. Many local people are furious with this situation and feel that Bromley Council have let them down as complaining seems to have no response.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Worsley Bridge Road, New Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti on Siebert road underpass, 4th July
item.description:
Graffiti on walls of underpass, needs painting over

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bramshot Avenue, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet
